title: Mercator Ocean - Ocean State Report 4 summary
organisation: Mercator Ocean, the French center for analysis and forecasting of the global ocean
summary: We worked with Mercator Ocean on the communication strategy for its annual Ocean State Report.
description: The report, based on data from the European Copernicus Earth Observation Programme, provides comprehensive state-of-the-art information on the ongoing changes and natural variations in the global ocean and European regional seas.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Mercator Ocean - Ocean State Report 4 summary": Design & Data GmbH

title: Mercator Ocean - SROCC social media video
organisation: Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS) managed by Mercator Ocean
summary: The Copernicus Marine Environment Monitoring Service (CMEMS) managed by Mercator Ocean provides full, free and open access to data & information related to our ocean.
description: Following the release of the Special Report on the Ocean and Cryosphere (SROCC) by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), we were commissioned to produce a social media video to demonstrate the use of Copernicus data and how it provides vital information needed to adapt to the changing ocean environment.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Mercator Ocean - SROCC social media video": Design & Data GmbH

title: Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Windy
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: Design & Data worked with Copernicus ECMWF to communicate case studies, which demonstrate the impact and benefits of Copernicus for service users and European citizens.
description: Windy is a leading Czech start-up that enables approximately 800 000 people per day to see information about different weather parameters laid over a world map. It provides global air quality information provided by the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) implemented by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) on behalf of the European Union.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Windy": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - PhiWeek Bootcamp 2019
organisation: European Space Agency
summary: Organised by Design & Data, the PhiWeek Bootcamp was hosted in 2019 by the European Space Agency (ESA) in the framework of Phi-Week.
description: It is was intensive 5-day event, guiding you through the design sprint process and answering critical questions in the prototyping and testing of ideas relating to Earth Observation.
production_credit: Complete Production of "ESA - PhiWeek Bootcamp 2019": Design & Data GmbH

title: Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Breezometer
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: Design & Data worked with Copernicus ECMWF to communicate case studies, which demonstrate the impact and benefits of Copernicus for service users and European citizens.
description: Breezometer provides a source for air quality data, enabling individuals to make better health decisions based on the air pollution levels at their specific location and individual sensitivities. It uses air quality information provided by the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) implemented by the European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF) on behalf of the European Union.
production_credit: Complete Production of "Copernicus ECMWF - CAMS Breezometer": Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Vortex - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: Vortex use data from C3S for its online wind modelling service. Vortex provides tools for wind farm development, among others that enable critical decision-making and easy-to-use solutions.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - DiscovAir app - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: DiscovAir use data from CAMS to help tourists prepare appropriately to enjoy their experience in Greece and Cyprus. In combination with personal health information, DiscovAir recommends an optimal time to visit any given location, based on its air quality, UV index, pollen, and temperature available. An app will be available to the public shortly and is currently being evaluated by beta-testers.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Riga airTEXT - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: A series of videos highlighting different use cases were shown to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018 to illustrate the important role data from Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF: CAMS and C3S.
description: Riga airTEXT use CAMS data to provide air quality monitoring to Latvian residents. Users can track air quality, UV, pollen and temperature forecasts across the city of Riga online or through text alerts.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU.
description: Presented to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018, this video presents how these services offer a way to better understand our planet, protect and sustain our environment and respond to climate change and challenges in key sectors.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Copernicus ECMWF Overview - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU.
description: Presented to the European Parliament at the European State of the Climate event on 10 April 2018, the video demonstrates how with decades of experience in delivering weather and climate modelling, ECMWF have the expertise, facilities, and structure in place to process and make raw Copernicus data accessible to Europe’s scientific, business and policy sectors.
production_credit: Complete Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Trailer - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: On the 10th April 2018, the Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF were presented at a high-level event under the patronage of the European Parliament.
description: The aim of the event was to raise awareness and support of the Copernicus Programme among MEPs and showcase the European State of the Climate report. This trailer was shown to introduce the event.
production_credit: Complete Production:: Design & Data GmbH

title: ECMWF - Background Slideshow - European State of the Climate event 10.04.2018
organisation: European Centre for Medium-Range Weather Forecasts (ECMWF)
summary: On the 10th April 2018, the Copernicus services implemented by ECMWF were presented at a high-level event under the patronage of the European Parliament.
description: The ECMWF operates the Copernicus Atmosphere Monitoring Service (CAMS) and the Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) on behalf of the EU. This slideshow was presented to MEPs to illustrate the benefits of Copernicus’ freely accessible environmental data which help us to better understand our planet and inform us in our decision-making. The slideshow is without audio.
production_credit: Complete Production:: Design & Data GmbH

title: DLR - INNOspace Weekend 2017 Event Video
organisation: DLR Space Administration
summary: Event video for the INNOspace Weekend 2017, a new event hosted by the DLR Space Administration.
description: In the course of the INNOspace initiative the event was realised as a 3-day intensive weekend of activities where aspiring entrepreneurs could unlock their creative potential, invent the future and build a startup in just 60 hours.
production_credit: Complete production by Design & Data GmbH for DLR Space Administration.

title: DLR - INNOspace Weekend Teaser
organisation: DLR - Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e.V.
summary: 30 second trailer for the INNOspace Weekend, a new event hosted by the DLR Space Administration.
description: In the course of the INNOspace initiative is a 3-day intensive weekend of activities where aspiring entrepreneurs could unlock their creative potential, invent the future and build a startup in just 60 hours.
production_credit: Animations, design and production by Design & Data GmbH for DLR - Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e.V..

title: ESA - Living with space 4.0
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Space technology surrounds us, sometimes without us even noticing – from the moment we wake up to when we sleep at night.
description: Every day space benefits us all, depicted in our latest video.
production_credit: Animations, design, storyboard and production by Design & Data GmbH for ESA- European Space Agency.

title: EUMETSAT and Africa: An Overview
organisation: EUMETSAT
summary: In the first of a series of five videos showcasing EUMETSAT's activities in Africa, we get an overview of their work in the region.
description: We explore how EUMETSAT supports the African community to make the best use of meteorological satellite data through a number of projects and initiatives in cooperation with the European Union, the African Union and the World Meteorological Organization. These in turn enable African meteorologists to give severe weather warnings to local communities, enable them to manage their water and agriculture, and help mitigate the effects of natural hazards and climate change.
production_credit: Complete Production of "EUMETSAT and Africa - An Overview": Design & Data GmbH

title: EUMETSAT and Africa: Food Security
organisation: EUMETSAT
summary: In the second of a series of five videos showcasing EUMETSAT's activities in Africa, we look at the importance of food security in the region.
description: We see how satellite data provided by EUMETSAT is important for agriculture and fisheries in Africa (e.g. land surface, rainfall, vegetation, evapo-transpiration, soil moisture, sea surface temperature and ocean colour). In combination with ground observations, users can provide vital information to the policymakers in agriculture and fisheries, reinforcing food security in Africa.
production_credit: Complete Production of "EUMETSAT and Africa - Food Security": Design & Data GmbH

title: Rosetta Strato Charity Campaign
organisation: Design & Data GmbH with Universe Awareness (UNAWE)
summary: We want to help continue Rosetta's legacy, by sharing her heroic story, using the plush toy as an interactive teaching resource to educate school children around the world in astronomy and space sciences.
description: Design & Data GmbH has partnered with Universe Awareness (UNAWE) to help train teachers and send them one of these lovely toys for use in the classroom. With your pledge we will send a toy to where it's needed most: underprivileged schools around the world. We hope we can count on your support! Your pledge can enter you into a raffle to win the unique Rosetta Strato toy that ventured to the stratosphere.
production_credit: Production: Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Making and testing for Mercury
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: The ESA-JAXA BepiColombo mission to Mercury must withstand extremely harsh conditions while in orbit around the innermost planet of our Solar System.
production_credit: Complete production of "Making and testing for Mercury": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Securing Aeolus against atomic oxygen
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Low-orbiting space missions can be susceptible to atomic oxygen erosion.
description: ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory was tasked with investigating the resilience of beta cloth fibre.
production_credit: Complete production of "Securing Aelous Against Atomic Oxygen": Design & Data GmbH

title: ESA - Securing Aeolus against atomic oxygen - Subtitled
summary: Low-orbiting space missions can be susceptible to atomic oxygen erosion.
description: ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory was tasked with investigating the resilience of beta cloth fibre.

title: ESA - Getting Vega’s launch tower moving
organisation: ESA - European Space Agency
summary: Once its mobile launch gantry is moved away, then Europe’s Vega launcher is primed for lift-off – except for the time the stuck gantry refused to move.
description: Solving the problem and getting the gantry mobile again was one of the more unusual tasks of ESA’s Materials and Electrical Components Laboratory.
production_credit: Complete production of "Getting Vega's launch tower moving": Design & Data GmbH

title: Rosetta Plush Toy Reaches Stratosphere
summary: We asked ourselves, could the Rosetta toy also survive the harsh conditions of space?
description: Every space mission involves a rigorous period of modelling and testing; the Rosetta Strato mission was no different. Our aim was to recreate a key scene from the Rosetta cartoon series with the toy’s solar panels deployed. There was every risk however this might not work. On 17th March 2016 at 10:40 CET from Gniezno in Poland we decided to launch. With steady conditions and clear, blue skies we released the toy attached to a stratospheric balloon where it reached over 30km in altitude. But could we capture the key scene of the solar panels deployed? And could we recover the toy? Following a short search, the toy was successfully recovered from its space adventure; it landed on a tree-shaped comet near Miąskowo, Poland. We’re very happy to finally reveal the footage of its journey to the stratosphere and back to Earth. Enjoy!
credits: Many thanks to Blue Dot Solutions for their technical expertise in helping to make this mission a success. Music: Dvořák Symphony No. 9 III performed by Colombia University Orchestra. Production by Design & Data GmbH.

title: #1 - Commercial Opportunities for Human Spaceflight in Europe
summary: This episode explores how Europe is building commercial opportunities in human spaceflight, from access to the International Space Station to new services in low Earth orbit.
description:
As a new era of human spaceflight began with NASA’s first Commercial Crew launch on 27 May, this session looks at Europe’s contribution to human spaceflight and how commercial initiatives are enabling services to the International Space Station and beyond.

Panellists:

Dr Hilde Stenuit – ICE Cubes Business Developer, Space Applications Services
Dr Hilde Stenuit is a seasoned research and team leader for Space Applications Services. She received her M.Sc. in Mathematics & Applied Physics and her Ph.D. in Plasma-Astrophysics from the Katholieke Universiteit Leuven (KUL). Since then she has worked with Space Applications Services. After working as operations specialist for International Space Station modules and spacecraft (ATV), she coordinated research operations on National Soyuz missions. She later supported ESA and spent more than a decade in ESA’s ISS Mission Science Office, doing scientific coordination and research planning for ISS experiments. She now helps create opportunities for commercial access to LEO through ICE Cubes, the first European commercial ISS service.

Juergen Schlutz – Exploration Coordinator, German Aerospace Center (DLR)
Juergen is an aerospace engineer and project manager with a strong background in systems and concurrent engineering for human space missions and space exploration. He combines his passion for space with a broad understanding of programme and technical aspects, intercultural experience, and communication and managerial skills. Alongside his work, he has initiated and managed educational and outreach activities such as Space Station Design Workshops, Yuri’s Night, and Space Generation Congresses.

Yannick Jégo – Business Development & Sales Manager, Airbus Defence & Space
Yannick has over 20 years of experience in the space business, starting as an engineer on the Ariane 4 launcher programme, then on the development of the Ariane 5 launcher and the manufacturing of cryo-engines. He later moved into business development and the incubation of new products and services, and helped set up two companies. Today he leads sales of Airbus’s self-financed ISS external platform Bartolomeo, a pioneer in the commercialisation of the ISS.

title: #2 - Space Clusters Across Europe
summary: This episode looks at how European space clusters support startups, collaboration, and regional growth across the wider space ecosystem.
description:
Space is a growing market with many startups emerging. Business Incubation Centres are helping inspire and work with entrepreneurs to turn ideas into fully commercial startup companies. In this webinar, we hear perspectives from space clusters across Europe in the UK, France, Portugal, and Luxembourg.

Dr Joanna Hart – Harwell Space Cluster Development Manager, UKRI - Science and Technology Facilities Council
Dr Joanna Hart is the Harwell Space Cluster Development Manager, working with stakeholders to strengthen the Harwell Space Cluster by attracting new companies to Harwell Campus and helping those already there to grow. Previously, she was Head of Evaluation and Impact at Satellite Applications Catapult and spent nine years in investment banking focused on the telecoms sector.

Romain Erny – Director, Mobility and Space, Choose Paris Region
Romain leads the Paris Region’s consulting practice for the urban mobility and new space sectors. He has more than 10 years of experience advising companies looking to expand and connect to the regional ecosystem. He is co-founder of the Space Paris Region initiative.

Dr Chiara Manfletti – President, Portuguese Space Agency
Chiara Manfletti has dual Italian and German nationality. She holds degrees in aeronautical engineering, Space Studies, and history, plus a PhD from the German University of Aachen. She worked for the German Aerospace Center as a research engineer in liquid rocket propulsion, then joined the European Space Agency as Programme Advisor to the Director General. She is now President of the Portuguese Space Agency, Portugal Space.

Sarah Fleischer – Economic Advisor, Luxembourg Space Agency
Sarah Fleischer is the Economic Advisor at the Luxembourg Space Agency. She develops financial solutions for Luxembourg’s growing space ecosystem, including the early-stage space fund Orbital Ventures. She is also helping set up the Business Division and Startup Incubator for the forthcoming European Space Resource Innovation Centre, and teaches Entrepreneurial Space Finance at the University of Luxembourg.

title: #3 - Investment in Space
summary: This episode examines investment in the space sector, including startup funding, commercialisation, and the shift toward private-sector leadership.
description:
We are in an exciting and vibrant new era of space. The sector has been heavily impacted by advancements across the entire value chain, from lower launch costs and new technology in upstream systems to cloud computing and data processing in downstream services. Morgan Stanley Research estimates that the global space industry could generate revenue of more than $1 trillion by 2040, up from $350 billion currently. The industry has shifted from government-led initiatives toward commercialisation and greater private-sector participation. In recent years, many new space startups have emerged, alongside public-private partnerships and shared leadership between government and private entities. According to Bryce Space and Technology, 2019 was a record year for startup space investment, with more than $5.7 billion reported. This episode explores current trends in the sector, opportunities ahead in investment, and how coronavirus may affect the future of space business.

Manny Shar – Head of Analytics, Bryce Space and Technology
Manny Shar leads analytics at Bryce Space & Technology and has managed consulting engagements for government and commercial clients. He helped build Bryce’s presence in Europe and has worked on projects ranging from early-stage investment to commercial model development in the space and satellite sector.

Claudia Mendzil – Programme Director, Seraphim Space Camp
Claudia is the Programme Director of Seraphim Space Camp, the UK’s first accelerator programme dedicated to the rapidly growing SpaceTech startup sector. The programme helps seed-stage companies become investment-ready and connect with corporate partners. Since launching in 2018, it has supported 35 startups, which have collectively raised £30M in seed investment and created more than 100 jobs.

Tala Atie – Senior Consultant, PwC Advisory France
Tala Atie is a senior consultant in PwC Advisory France and part of the PwC dedicated space practice. Her work covers space market assessments, socio-economic studies, space policy, and regulatory matters. She has supported the European Space Agency, the European Commission, space agencies worldwide, and the space industry across EO, exploration, and related domains.

title: ESOcast 64: First Ring System Around Asteroid
summary: Observations from South America, including ESO’s La Silla Observatory, revealed that the asteroid Chariklo is surrounded by two narrow rings, making it the smallest known object with rings and only the fifth body in the Solar System known to have this feature.
description: This ESOcast presents the surprise discovery that the remote asteroid Chariklo is surrounded by two dense and narrow rings. The finding came from observations at multiple sites in South America, including ESO’s La Silla Observatory, and shows that Chariklo is the smallest object found to have rings. The origin of the rings is still unknown, but they may have formed from a collision that created a disc of debris.
credits: Credit: ESO. Visual design and editing: Martin Kornmesser and Luis Calçada. Editing: Herbert Zodet. Web and technical support: Mathias André and Raquel Yumi Shida. Written by Ryan Laird and Richard Hook. Presented by Joe Liske (Dr. J). Narration: Sara Mendes da Costa. Music: John Stanford. Footage and photos: ESO, Felipe Braga Ribas, Luis Calçada, Martin Kornmesser, José Francisco Salgado, Alexandre Santerne, NASA, ESA and M. Showalter. Directed by Herbert Zodet. Executive producer: Lars Lindberg Christensen.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #PreparingForSpace panel
summary: This panel explores how satellites, rovers, and humans are prepared for space exploration, covering mission design, testing, operations, astronaut training, and launch readiness.
description: This panel session is focused on the theme #PreparingForSpace and looks at the ground-based work needed to prepare satellites, rovers, and humans for space exploration. The discussion covers mission design, testing, in-situ and on-Earth operations, qualification, astronaut training, and launch. It features speakers from RAL Space, the Austrian Space Forum, ESA, and Airbus Defence and Space Engineering Academy, alongside the SpaceUp Leicester community.
credits: This session was kindly sponsored by HE Space. It is part of the SpaceUp LIVE series organised by SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space.

title: Loretta Whitesides Keynote #SpaceUpLive
summary: Loretta Whitesides shares a keynote about space exploration, leadership, and the belief that seemingly impossible goals can become reality.
description: This keynote from the #PreparingForSpace edition of SpaceUp Live features Loretta Whitesides, SpaceKind founder and Yuri’s Night co-creator. She speaks about space exploration, Earth connection, and how leadership and imagination can help people reach ambitious goals. Her talk frames space not just as a destination, but as a way to inspire people to take on challenges that once seemed impossible.
credits: Loretta Whitesides is introduced as SpaceKind Founder, Yuri’s Night co-creator, future Astronaut, former Zero-G Flight Director, and deep ocean explorer.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #PreparingForSpace HESpace sponsor talk
summary: This sponsor talk introduces HE Space through Agnieszka Iwanczuk’s background in recruitment, people-focused work, and candidate preparation.
description: Agnieszka Iwanczuk, Senior Recruiter at HE Space, gives the sponsor talk for the #PreparingForSpace edition of SpaceUp Live. She describes her experience in international recruitment and human resources, along with her focus on understanding people’s needs, building relationships, and preparing candidates for interviews. The talk connects the sponsor’s role to the wider SpaceUp community.
credits: This session was sponsored by HE Space and presented within the SpaceUp LIVE #PreparingForSpace programme.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #1
summary: The first SpaceUp LIVE brought together a global online space unconference with talks, discussions, and a panel on how COVID-19 could affect the space industry.
description: This first SpaceUp LIVE event was organised by SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space with SpaceUp Foundation. It created an online version of the global space unconference, allowing presenters and attendees to share ideas remotely through scheduled live sessions and discussion panels. The programme included talks on Earth observation, Mars, funding, astro-tourism, inclusion, astronaut training, satellite missions, and the impact of COVID-19 on the space industry.
credits: Presented by the SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space community in collaboration with SpaceUp Foundation.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #2
summary: This second SpaceUp LIVE event continued the online unconference format with talks on virtual community, microgravity, Mars analog stations, satellite tracking, and NewSpace in Africa.
description: SpaceUp LIVE #2 expanded the online SpaceUp Leicester format with a new round of talks and a panel discussion. Speakers explored topics such as virtual space communities, microgravity test platforms, Mars greenhouse concepts, planetary and orbital tracking tools, and the growth of NewSpace ecosystems in Africa. The event kept the collaborative, open session format that defined the SpaceUp LIVE series.
credits: Organised by SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space with support from the SpaceUp Foundation.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #3 - World Space Week Edition
summary: This World Space Week edition of SpaceUp LIVE offered an open online programme for talks, discussions, and community participation around space topics.
description: The third SpaceUp LIVE event was held as a World Space Week edition, continuing the global online space unconference format. It brought together community members for short talks, open discussion, and informal exchange around a wide range of space-related ideas. The session maintained the accessible, interactive style that made the series a community hub for space enthusiasts.
credits: Organised by SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space in collaboration with SpaceUp Foundation.

title: SpaceUp LIVE #4 - #PreparingForSpace
summary: This edition of SpaceUp LIVE focused on #PreparingForSpace and brought together the community for online talks and discussion.
description: SpaceUp LIVE #4 returned to the #PreparingForSpace theme with another online edition of the global space unconference. The event followed the familiar SpaceUp LIVE structure of scheduled talks, live discussion, and community participation, all hosted remotely. It continued the series’ role in connecting people interested in space across different countries and disciplines.
credits: Organised by SpaceUp Leicester and R&R Space with support from SpaceUp Foundation.
